DESCRIPTION: Profile parameter rec/client should be set to the value ALL
NAME: Activate/Deactivate table auditing

DESCRIPTION: Additional authorization checks should be enabled for transactional (tRFC) and queued RFC (qRFC) calls to mitigate a privilege escalation vulnerability in AS ABAP during RFC playbacks. This can be enabled by setting the profile parameter rfc/authCheckInPlayback to 1 . Note that the vulnerability is only applicable to kernel versions 789, 793, 914 and 915. The Status of the finding can be set to Irrelevant if the kernel version is not relevant
NAME: Authorization checks for RFC playbacks

DESCRIPTION: Profile parameter auth/rfc_authority_check should be set to the value 1 or 6
NAME: Authorization checks for RFMs

DESCRIPTION: Profile parameter login/password_downwards_compatibility should be set to the value 0
NAME: Backward Compatibility of Passwords

DESCRIPTION: Profile parameter login/no_automatic_user_sapstar should be set to the value 1
NAME: Control of the automatic login user SAP*

DESCRIPTION:
Profile parameter ssl/client_ciphersuites should be set to the following value to activate TLS versions 1.2 and 1.3 only:

1584:PFS:HIGH::EC_X25519:EC_P256:EC_HIGH
NAME: Default configuration of available SSL/TLS cipher suites for the client side (outbound connections)

DESCRIPTION:
Profile parameter ssl/ciphersuites should be set to the following value to activate TLS versions 1.2 and 1.3 only:

1569:PFS:HIGH::EC_X25519:EC_P256:EC_HIGH
NAME: Default configuration of available SSL/TLS cipher suites for the server side (inbound connections)

DESCRIPTION: Profile parameter snc/enable should be set to the value 1
NAME: Enable SNC Module (Secure Network Communications)

DESCRIPTION: Profile parameter rsau/enable should be set to the value 1
NAME: Enable Security Audit

DESCRIPTION: Profile parameter ms/acl_info should be set to the value 0
NAME: Enable/disable external monitor

DESCRIPTION: Profile parameter login/ticket_only_by_https should be set to the value 1
NAME: Generate ticket that will only be sent via https

DESCRIPTION:
Profile  parameter login/password_hash_algorithm should be set to the following value:

encoding=RFC2307, algorithm=iSSHA-512, iterations=15000, saltsize=256
NAME: Hash procedure and the coding format for the calculation of new password hash values.

DESCRIPTION: Profile parameter system/secure_communication should be set to the value ON
NAME: Saving internal communication with SSL

DESCRIPTION:
Profile parameter snc/gssapi_lib should be set to the following value:

$(DIR_LIBRARY)$(DIR_SEP)$(FT_DLL_PREFIX)sapcrypto$(FT_DLL)
NAME: File name for external GSS-API shared library

DESCRIPTION: Profile parameter gw/accept_remote_trace_level should be set to the value 0
NAME: Gateway trace levels

DESCRIPTION: Profile parameter login/password_change_waittime should be set to the value 1 or higher
NAME: Wait time between two password changes

DESCRIPTION: Profile parameter icm/accept_remote_trace_level should be set to the value 0
NAME: Accept external switch of trace level

DESCRIPTION: Profile parameter snc/accept_insecure_cpic should be set to the value 0
NAME: Accept insecure incoming CPIC connections on an SNC-enabled application server

DESCRIPTION: Profile parameter snc/accept_insecure_gui should be set to the value 1 or U
NAME: Accept logins from unprotected (non-SNC-secured) SAP GUIs into an SNC-enabled application server

DESCRIPTION: Profile parameter snc/accept_insecure_rfc should be set to the value 1 or U
NAME: Accept non-secure incoming RFC connections on an SNC-enabled application server

DESCRIPTION: Access control list rules should be enforced for proxy connections based on the settings in secinfo and reginfo files. This is the recommended setting for enhanced security available as of Kernel 7.89. ACL checks should be enforced by setting the profile parameter gw/acl_mode_proxy to the value 1
NAME: Access control checks for proxy connections

DESCRIPTION:
Profile parameter service/admin_users should be set to the following value:

daaadm sapadm dacadm
NAME: Additional OS users authorized for system administration

DESCRIPTION: Maintenance of default authorization values and profiles should not be deactivated, This can be performed using the value setting Y for the profile parameter auth/no_check_in_some_cases
NAME: Authorization and role maintenance

DESCRIPTION: Authorization checks should be enforced for the object S_TCODE when transactions are called from other transactions using the CALL TRANSACTION command. This can be enforced using the value setting 2 or 3 for the profile parameter auth/check_calltransaction.
NAME: Authorization checks for S_TCODE

DESCRIPTION: Authorization checks should be enforced for database maintenance actions. This can be enforced using the value setting 1 for the profile parameter dbs/dba/ccms_maintenance.
NAME: Authorization checks for database maintenance actions

DESCRIPTION: The parameter login/disable_cpic should be set to 1 to disable incoming CPIC connections.  CPIC was used  to connect SAP R_3 systems with SAP R_2 systems. This functionality is no longer required in most SAP systems
NAME: CPIC Connections

DESCRIPTION:
Profile parameter icm/security_log should be set to the following value:

LOGFILE=dev_icm_sec-%y%m,LEVEL=3,MAXSIZEKB=10000,SWITCHTF=month
NAME: Configuration of ICM security log

DESCRIPTION: Profile parameter snc/data_protection/use should be set to the value 3
NAME: Default level of data protection for R/3-initiated connections.

DESCRIPTION: Profile parameter ixml/dtd_restriction should be set to the value prohibited
NAME: Default processing restriction of iXML for DTDs

DESCRIPTION: Profile parameter login/failed_user_auto_unlock should be set to the value 0
NAME: Disable automatic unlocking of locked user at midnight

DESCRIPTION: Password validity checks during single sign-on logons should be enforced using the value setting 1 for profile parameter login/password_change_for_SSO
NAME: Expired password checks with SSO

DESCRIPTION: Expired passwords for ICF users should be rejected using the value setting 1 for the profile parameter icf/reject_expired_passwd
NAME: Expired passwords for ICF users

DESCRIPTION: Expired passwords for RFC users should be rejected using the value setting 1 for the profile parameter rfc/reject_expired_passwd. This will enforce the use of system user types for RFC users since passwords do not expire for system users.
NAME: Expired passwords for RFC users

DESCRIPTION: HTTP debugging is permitted for external users. This should be blocked using setting 2 for profile parameter abap/ext_debugging_possible. Setting 0 is permitted for development and test environments
NAME: External HTTP debugging

DESCRIPTION:
Profile parameter gw/reg_info should be set to the following value:

$(DIR_GLOBAL)$(DIR_SEP)reginfo$(FT_DAT)
NAME: External security filename for gateway

DESCRIPTION: Profile parameter rdisp/accept_remote_trace_level should be set to the value 0
NAME: External switch of trace level

DESCRIPTION:
Profile parameter ms/acl_info should be set to the following value:

$(DIR_GLOBAL)$(DIR_SEP)$(FN_MS_ACL_INFO)
NAME: File with access control list for message server

DESCRIPTION: Profile parameter is/HTTP/show_detailed_errors should be set to the value FALSE
NAME: Form of HTTP error pages

DESCRIPTION:
Profile parameter gw/acl_mode should include the option and value ACTION=SsPXM if you are not using the Cybersecurity Extension for SAP.

If you are using the Cybersecurity Extension for SAP, the option and value should be ACTION=SsPXMZEXR.
NAME: Gateway Logging

DESCRIPTION:
Profile parameter gw/prxy_info should be set to the following value:

$(DIR_GLOBAL)$(DIR_SEP)prxyinfo$(FT_DAT)
NAME: Gateway proxy settings

DESCRIPTION: Profile parameter auth/object_disabling_active should be set to the value N
NAME: Global authorization checks

DESCRIPTION: Profile parameter is/HTTP/show_server_header should be set to the value FALSE
NAME: HTTP headers with the server entries

DESCRIPTION:
Profile parameter icm/HTTP/redirect_0 should be set to the following value:

PREFIX=/, FROM=*, FROMPROT=http, PROT=https, HOST=$(SAPLOCALHOSTFULL), PORT=443$$
NAME: HTTP redirect specification

DESCRIPTION: Sensitive information should not be disclosed in HTTP traces. This can be enforced using the value setting 1 or ON for profile parameter rdisp/TRACE_HIDE_SEC_DATA
NAME: HTTP trace information

DESCRIPTION: Parameter rdisp/gui_auto_logout should be set to a value between 1 and 7200
NAME: Inactive users are logged off after the specified period expires

DESCRIPTION: Profile parameter snc/data_protection/max should be set to the value 3
NAME: Limit on the data protection of Secure Network Communications

DESCRIPTION: Profile parameter icf/user_recheck should be set to the value 1
NAME: Logon data check for HTTP queries for an existing HTTP session

DESCRIPTION: Logon error settings should be securely maintained to prevent the disclosure of sensitive information. This can be enforced using the value setting 0 for login/show_detailed_errors
NAME: Logon error messages

DESCRIPTION: Profile parameter login/fails_to_user_lock should be set to a value between 1 and 6
NAME: Maximum number of failed attempts before user is locked

DESCRIPTION: Profile parameter login/min_password_digits should be set to the value 1 or higher
NAME: Minimum number of digits in passwords

DESCRIPTION: Profile parameter login/min_password_letters should be set to the value 1 or higher
NAME: Minimum number of letters in passwords

DESCRIPTION: Profile parameter login/min_password_lowercase should be set to the value 1 or higher
NAME: Minimum number of lowercase letters in passwords

DESCRIPTION: Profile parameter login/min_password_specials should be set to the value 1 or higher
NAME: Minimum number of special characters in passwords

DESCRIPTION: Profile parameter login/min_password_uppercase should be set to the value 1 or higher
NAME: Minimum number of uppercase letters in passwords

DESCRIPTION: Profile parameter login/min_password_lng should be set to the value 15 or higher
NAME: Minimum password length

DESCRIPTION: Profile parameter snc/data_protection/min should be set to the value 3
NAME: Minimum required data protection for Secure Network Communications

DESCRIPTION: Profile parameter login/min_password_diff should be set to the value 1 or higher
NAME: Number of different characters: Old/new password

DESCRIPTION: Profile parameter login/fails_to_session_end should be set to a value between 1 and 3
NAME: Number of invalid login attempts until session ends

DESCRIPTION: Profile parameter login/password_compliance_to_current_policy should be set to the value 1
NAME: Password must fulfill the current password rules

DESCRIPTION: Profile parameter snc/permit_insecure_start should be set to the value 0 or 1
NAME: Permission to start insecure programs when snc is enabled

DESCRIPTION: Profile parameter login/create_sso2_ticket_ticket should be set to the value 2 or 3
NAME: Permit generation of SSO tickets

DESCRIPTION: Profile parameter login/accept_sso2_ticket should be set to the value 1
NAME: Permit or prevent logon with an SSO ticket

DESCRIPTION: Profile parameter service/admin_users should be set to the value SDEFAULT
NAME: Protected Web service functions requiring user authorization

DESCRIPTION: Positive whitelists should be configured to control the execution of RFC callbacks in the calling system from called systems. Once configured, whitelists should be enabled using the setting 3 for profile parameter rfc/callback_security_method
NAME: RFC callback whitelists

DESCRIPTION: Profile parameter gw/acl_mode should be set to the value 1
NAME: Remote external and registered servers

DESCRIPTION: Starting of remote CPIC programs on remote hosts should be disabled or restricted to secure remote shell. This can be enabled using the setting DISABLED for profile parameter gw/rem_start
NAME: Remote start of CPIC programs

DESCRIPTION: Profile parameter icm/HTTPS/verify_client should be set to the value 1
NAME: Require SSL Client Certificate

DESCRIPTION: SAP GUI scripting should be disabled using the value setting FALSE for profile parameter sapgui/user_scripting
NAME: SAP GUI scripting

DESCRIPTION:
Profile parameter rfc/allowoldticket4tt should be set to the value no.

The setting is recommended to mitigate a critical capture-replay vulnerability in SAP NetWeaver AS ABAP. Refer to SAP Note 3089413 for further details.
NAME: Secure Trusted-Trusting communication

DESCRIPTION: Profile parameter gw/reg_no_conn_info should be set to the value 255
NAME: Security levels for sec_info and reg_info

DESCRIPTION:
Profile parameter gw/sec_info should be set to the following value:

$(DIR_GLOBAL)$(DIR_SEP)secinfo$(FT_DAT)
NAME: Security levels for sec_info and reg_info

DESCRIPTION: Profile parameter icf/set_HTTPonly_flag_on_cookies should be set to the value 0
NAME: Set HTTPonly-Flag for Cookies

DESCRIPTION: Profile parameter login/password_history_size should be set to the value 15 or higher
NAME: Size of Password History

DESCRIPTION:
Profile parameter icm/HTTP/logging_client_0 should be set to the following value:

PREFIX=/,LOGFILE=$(DIR_LOGGING)$(DIR_SEP)http-client-$(SAPSYSTEMNAME)-$(SAPLOCALHOST)-%y%m,MAXSIZEKB=10000,SWITCHTF=month, LOGFORMAT=%t %a %u - "%r" %s %b %Lms %{Host}i %P %w1 %w2%y1 %y2
NAME: Specify HTTP client log

DESCRIPTION:
Profile parameter icm/HTTP/logging_0 should include the option and value LOGFORMAT=%t %a %u - "%r" %s %b %Lms %{Host}i %P %w1 %w2%y1 %y2 if you are not using the Cybersecurity Extension for SAP.

If you are using the Cybersecurity Extension for SAP, the option and value should be LOGFORMAT=CLF for the Common Log Format.
NAME: Specify HTTP logging in ICM

DESCRIPTION: System-specific authorization checks should be enforced for database maintenance actions. This can be enforced using the value setting 1 for the profile parameter dbs/dba/ccms_security_level. The attributes DBA_DBHOST, DBA_DBSID, DBA_DBUSER and ACTVT should be maintained for authorization object S_DBCON for each user
NAME: System-specific authorization checks for database maintenance actions

DESCRIPTION: Profile parameter icm/HTTPS/client_sni_enabled should be set to the value TRUE
NAME: TLS extension for client-side Server Name Indication (SNI)

DESCRIPTION: Profile parameter ssl/client_sni_enabled should be set to the value TRUE
NAME: TLS extension for client-side Server Name Indication (SNI)

DESCRIPTION: Profile parameter login/ticket_only_to_host should be set to the value 1
NAME: Ticket is only be sent back to creating host

DESCRIPTION: Profile parameter login/password_max_idle_initial should be set to a value between 1 and 7
NAME: Validity Period of Unused Initial Passwords

DESCRIPTION: Profile parameter login/password_max_idle_productive should be set to a value between 30 and 90
NAME: Validity period for unused productive passwords

DESCRIPTION: Profile parameter login/password_expiration_time to a value should be set between 30 and 90
NAME: Validity period of passwords (in days)

DESCRIPTION: The FORMTORFC ICF service should be deactivated due to known security vulnerabilities with the service.
NAME: FORMTORFC ICF service

DESCRIPTION: The IDOC ICF service should be deactivated due to known security vulnerabilities with the service.
NAME: IDOC ICF service

DESCRIPTION: The IDOC_XML ICF service should be deactivated due to known security vulnerabilities with the service.
NAME: IDOC_XML ICF service

DESCRIPTION: The ms/acl_info file should not contain a line with HOST=*
NAME: Message server hardening (acl_info content)

DESCRIPTION:
The reginfo file must contain the following content:

#VERSION=2
P TP=* HOST=local
P TP=* HOST=internal
P TP=<authorized_tp> HOST=<authorized_host>
NAME: RFC gateway hardening (reginfo)

DESCRIPTION:
The secinfo file must contain the following content:

#VERSION=2
#
P USER=* USER-HOST=local HOST=local TP=*
P USER=* USER-HOST=local HOST=internal TP=*
P USER=* USER-HOST=internal HOST=local TP=
NAME: RFC gateway hardening (secinfo)

DESCRIPTION: The SOAP RFC ICF service should be deactivated due to known security vulnerabilities with the service.
NAME: SOAP RFC ICF service

DESCRIPTION: The default password for the standard SAP* user should be changed, the user should be locked in all clients, and the user should be assigned to the SUPER user group
NAME: Secure SAP* user

DESCRIPTION: The WEBRFC ICF service should be deactivated due to known security vulnerabilities with the service.
NAME: WEBRFC ICF service

DESCRIPTION: The user table USH02 should be assigned to the table authorization group SPWD
NAME: Authorization group for table USH02

DESCRIPTION: The user table USH02_ARC_TMP should be assigned to the table authorization group SPWD
NAME: Authorization group for table USH02_ARC_TMP

DESCRIPTION: The user table USR02 should be assigned to the table authorization group SPWD
NAME: Authorization group for table USR02

DESCRIPTION: The user table USRPWDHISTORY should be assigned to the table authorization group SPWD
NAME: Authorization group for table USRPWDHISTORY

DESCRIPTION: The user table VUSER001 should be assigned to the table authorization group SPWD
NAME: Authorization group for table VUSER001

DESCRIPTION: The user table VUSR02_PWD should be assigned to the table authorization group SPWD
NAME: Authorization group for table VUSR02_PWD

DESCRIPTION:
Productive clients should include the settings below to prevent unauthorized changes.  

Changes and Transports for Client-Specific Objects : No changes allowed (CCCORACTIV=2)
Cross-Client Object Changes : No Changes to Repository and cross-client Customizing obj (CCNOCLIIND=3)
Protection: Protection level 1 : No overwriting (CCCOPYLOCK=X or L)
CATT and eCATT Restrictions : eCAtt and CATT Not Allowed (CCIMAILDIS=NULL)
NAME: Client settings

DESCRIPTION:
Profile parameter icm/server_port_4 should be set to the following value:

PROT=HTTPS,PORT=204$$,TIMEOUT=300,PROCTIMEOUT=600,SSLCONFIG=ssl_config_<m>
NAME: ICM server specification for HTTPS (icm/server_port)

DESCRIPTION:
Profile parameter icm/ssl_config_1 should be set to the following value:

CRED=SAPSSLS_SAPADM.pse,VCLIENT=1
NAME: ICM server specification for HTTPS (icm/ssl_config)

DESCRIPTION: The default password for the standard DDIC user should be changed and the user should be assigned to the SUPER user group
NAME: Secure DDIC user

DESCRIPTION: The standard user EARLYWATCH should not exist in any client
NAME: Secure EARLYWATCH user

DESCRIPTION: The default password for the standard SAPCPIC user should be changed or the user should be deleted if not in use
NAME: Secure SAPCPIC user

DESCRIPTION: The default password for the standard TMSADM user should be changed and the user should only exist in client 000
NAME: Secure TMSADM user

DESCRIPTION: The table SSF_PSE_D MUST be assigned to the authorization group SPSE
NAME: Secure cryptographic keys

DESCRIPTION: System change settings should be set to Not Modifiable
NAME: System changes

DESCRIPTION: The BSP_MODEL ICF service should be deactivated due to known security vulnerabilities with the service.
NAME: BSP_MODEL ICF service

DESCRIPTION: The BSP_VERI ICF service should be deactivated due to known security vulnerabilities with the service.
NAME: BSP_VERI ICF service

DESCRIPTION: The CERTMAP ICF service should be deactivated due to known security vulnerabilities with the service.
NAME: CERTMAP ICF service

DESCRIPTION: The CERTREQ ICF service should be deactivated due to known security vulnerabilities with the service.
NAME: CERTREQ ICF service

DESCRIPTION: Client 001 should be removed to reduce the attack surface
NAME: Delete client 001

DESCRIPTION: Client 066 should be removed to reduce the attack surface
NAME: Delete client 066

DESCRIPTION: The ECHO ICF service should be deactivated due to known security vulnerabilities with the service.
NAME: ECHO ICF service

DESCRIPTION: The ERROR ICF service should be deactivated due to known security vulnerabilities with the service.
NAME: ERROR ICF service

DESCRIPTION: The HTMLB_SAMPLES ICF service ICF service should be deactivated due to known security vulnerabilities with the service.
NAME: HTMLB_SAMPLES ICF service

DESCRIPTION: The ICF service should be deactivated due to known security vulnerabilities with the service.
NAME: ICF service

DESCRIPTION: The IT00 ICF service ICF service should be deactivated due to known security vulnerabilities with the service.
NAME: IT00 ICF service

DESCRIPTION: The IT01 ICF service ICF service should be deactivated due to known security vulnerabilities with the service.
NAME: IT01 ICF service

DESCRIPTION: The IT02 ICF service ICF service should be deactivated due to known security vulnerabilities with the service.
NAME: IT02 ICF service

DESCRIPTION: The IT03 ICF service ICF service should be deactivated due to known security vulnerabilities with the service.
NAME: IT03 ICF service

DESCRIPTION: The IT04 ICF service ICF service should be deactivated due to known security vulnerabilities with the service.
NAME: IT04 ICF service

DESCRIPTION: The IT05 ICF service ICF service should be deactivated due to known security vulnerabilities with the service.
NAME: IT05 ICF service

DESCRIPTION: The ITMVC2 ICF service ICF service should be deactivated due to known security vulnerabilities with the service.
NAME: ITMVC2 ICF service

DESCRIPTION: The ITS CERTMAP ICF service should be deactivated due to known security vulnerabilities with the service.
NAME: ITS CERTMAP ICF service

DESCRIPTION: The ITS CERTREQ ICF service should be deactivated due to known security vulnerabilities with the service.
NAME: ITS CERTREQ ICF service

DESCRIPTION: The ITSM ICF service ICF service should be deactivated due to known security vulnerabilities with the service.
NAME: ITSM ICF service

DESCRIPTION: The REPORT ICF service should be deactivated due to known security vulnerabilities with the service.
NAME: REPORT ICF service

DESCRIPTION:
The prxyinfo file should contain the following content:

P SOURCE=local,internal DEST=*
NAME: RFC gateway hardening (prxyinfo)

DESCRIPTION: The SBSPEXT_HTMLB ICF service ICF service should be deactivated due to known security vulnerabilities with the service.
NAME: SBSPEXT_HTMLB ICF service

DESCRIPTION: The SBSPEXT_PHTMLB ICF service ICF service should be deactivated due to known security vulnerabilities with the service.
NAME: SBSPEXT_PHTMLB ICF service

DESCRIPTION: The SBSPEXT_TABLE ICF service ICF service should be deactivated due to known security vulnerabilities with the service.
NAME: SBSPEXT_TABLE ICF service

DESCRIPTION: The SBSPEXT_XHTMLB ICF service ICF service should be deactivated due to known security vulnerabilities with the service.
NAME: SBSPEXT_XHTMLB ICF service

DESCRIPTION: The SYSTEM_PRIVATE ICF service ICF service should be deactivated due to known security vulnerabilities with the service.
NAME: SYSTEM_PRIVATE ICF service

DESCRIPTION: The SYSTEM_PUBLIC ICF service ICF service should be deactivated due to known security vulnerabilities with the service.
NAME: SYSTEM_PUBLIC ICF service

DESCRIPTION: The XRFC ICF service should be deactivated due to known security vulnerabilities with the service.
NAME: XRFC ICF service

DESCRIPTION: The XRFC_TEST ICF service should be deactivated due to known security vulnerabilities with the service.
NAME: XRFC_TEST ICF service
